news.headline: Earthworms studied for vermifiltration at Vermitech Innovation Center in Walla Walla for sustainable wastewater treatment.
news.summary: In Walla Walla, Washington, on March 20, 2025, an innovative approach to wastewater treatment is gaining attention. Industries and municipalities are turning to earthworms as a sustainable solution for this crucial process. Through vermifiltration, which harnesses the power of these creatures, significant improvements have been observed. This method is proving to be reliable and effective, highlighting the potential of earthworms in addressing environmental challenges. Stay tuned for more updates on this eco-friendly initiative.

news.headline: Nektar Therapeutics will participate in H.C. Wainwright 3rd Annual Autoimmune & Inflammatory Disease Virtual Conference.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, Nektar Therapeutics (Nasdaq: NKTR) disclosed plans to webcast its involvement in the H.C. Wainwright 3rd Annual Autoimmune & Inflammatory Disease Virtual Conference. The event will feature key insights and updates related to autoimmune and inflammatory conditions. This conference is pivotal for Nektar Therapeutics, a company listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol NKTR. It signifies an opportunity for the company to showcase its advancements in the field and engage with stakeholders. The virtual format enables widespread reach, potentially impacting a large audience.

It signifies a transparent approach by Franco-Nevada Corporation in disclosing relevant information, aiming to ensure accountability and transparency in its operations.

news.summary: Micron Technology exceeded estimates for its fiscal Q2, fueled by robust performance in the data-center sector. This success resulted in a surge in MU stock prices following the announcement.

news.summary: Nike, a retailer in the Dow Jones, exceeded expectations for its Q3 results in 2025 on Thursday. Following this news, Nike's stock surged in after-hours trading. The performance was highlighted in an article titled "Nike Stock Climbs, Earnings Muscle Out Weak Expectations" on Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: European dermatology company sought regulatory support from DelveInsight to navigate CDSCO framework for market-ready product launch in India.
news.summary: A prominent European dermatology company recently utilized regulatory intelligence services to navigate the CDSCO framework in India. Their goal was to acquire a thorough understanding of the guidelines, requirements, and approval processes necessary to launch their product in the Indian market. This strategic move highlights the significance of seeking regulatory guidance when entering new markets.

news.headline: Bitcoin price surged to $87,453 but dipped to $83,655 post-Trump video at Digital Asset Summit. Traders cautious for a possible pullback to $73,700.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) surged to an intraday high of $87,453 before dropping to $83,655 after US President Trump's video appearance at the Digital Asset Summit in NY. Rumors of capital gains tax relief for certain cryptocurrencies were debunked. Trump reiterated not selling confiscated Bitcoins and urged Congress to enact stablecoin legislation quickly. Market reaction was negative as traders sold off. BTC may correct to $73,700 according to analyst Aksel Kibar. Recent price strength is partly due to FOMC minutes on March 19 and Fed Chair Powell's remarks on quantitative tightening. BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes noted QT's potential end on April 1, with caution on possible stock and BTC volatility. Futures markets heavily influenced BTC's recent price, but a resurgence in the BTC Coinbase premium suggests spot demand is picking up. Hayes advised caution and staying cashed up amidst potential volatility. President Trump's statement on making the US a "Bitcoin superpower" also impacted BTC price.

Investors can access the report online or request a physical copy for review. For further details, visit http://voc.q4web.com/home/default.aspx or www.sec.gov.

news.headline: Citadel's Wellington Fund decreases nearly 1% year on year: Source
VIQ Solutions ends strategic review process with board change. TSX: VQS provider of digital voice/video services.
news.summary: VIQ Solutions Inc., a global provider of secure digital voice and video capture technology, and transcription services, based in Mississauga, Ontario, has completed its strategic review process. The review, initiated on February 3, 2025, was led by a special committee of the company's board of directors. VIQ Solutions is listed on the TSX under the symbol VQS.

news.headline: Speculators scale back 2025 rate-cut bets as inflation fears rise ahead of March Fed meeting. Most favored scenario now foresees two cuts at 24% probability. Fed signals two cuts in 2025. Retail bettors cautious, financial markets slightly more hopeful.
news.summary: Speculators are reevaluating their expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts in 2025, with a focus on the March Federal Open Market Committee meeting. Traders on the Kalshi platform are moving their bets towards fewer than three rate cuts, particularly favoring the scenario of two cuts with a 24% implied probability. The chances of only one rate cut have increased to 19%. Retail bettors are also leaning towards two rate cuts, with a 24% implied probability. In December, the Fed's "dot plot" showed a median expectation of two rate cuts for 2025, down from four in September. While retail bettors are becoming more conservative, broader financial markets are still slightly more optimistic. Fed funds futures on the CME FedWatch tool currently indicate a full pricing of three rate cuts by December 2024, with a 35% probability.

news.headline: Economic fears rise as uncertainty plagues markets. Index shows concerns but 5 recession-proof stocks remain strong.
news.summary: The market is facing uncertainty with souring sentiment data as markets tumble and tariff policies lack consistency. The St Louis Fed's US Economic Policy Uncertainty Index hit its highest level since December 2020 while the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers dropped nearly 10% from January to February and is down 17% year-over-year. The NFIB Small Business Optimism Index fell, and the NFIB Uncertainty Index rose to its second-highest recorded level, mentioning tariff uncertainty. Inflation expectations for 2025 are rising, putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to balance growth and price stability. U.S. stocks are lagging global markets, causing investor unease. Five stocks with solid fundamentals in sectors that perform well during economic slowdowns are suggested. Republic Services Inc. is highlighted for its waste management services, contrasting its performance with Waste Management Inc. (NYSE:WM). Republic Services (NYSE:RSG) offers integrated waste services with competitive financial ratios and a stock increase of over 27% in the last 12 months. The article recommends keeping an eye on Republic Services Inc. due to positive momentum and quality metrics.

news.summary: Transcontinental Realty Investors, Inc. (NYSE:TCI) has released its financial results for the quarter ending December 31, 2024. During this period, the company reported a net income attributable to common shares amounting to $0.1 million or $0.01 per diluted share. This is in comparison to a net loss of $2.6 million or $0.30 per diluted share for the same quarter in 2023. Notably, the total occupancy rate stood at 81% as of December 31, 2024, with the multifa... unit occupancy rate being at 94%.

news.headline: American Realty Investors, Inc. reports net loss of $0.2 million in Q4 2024, improved from $2.1 million in Q4 2023; total occupancy at 81%.
news.summary: In Dallas, American Realty Investors, Inc. (NYSE:ARL) has released its financial performance for the quarter ending December 31, 2024. During this period, there was a net loss attributable to common shares of $0.2 million, translating to $0.01 per diluted share. This is an improvement compared to the net loss of $2.1 million, equivalent to $0.13 per diluted share, reported for the same quarter in 2023. As of December 31, 2024, the total occupancy rate stood at 81%.

news.headline: Mayor Bowser selects Therme Group to develop Wellness Destination at Poplar Point, 15 acres for project, to boost economic growth.
news.summary: In Washington, Mayor Muriel Bowser and the Office of the Deputy Mayor for Planning and Economic Development revealed the selection of Therme Group's proposal to introduce Therme DC at Poplar Point in Ward 8. This project involves developing 15 acres of the 110-acre area into a cutting-edge health and wellbeing facility. The initiative aims to provide a distinctive experience for visitors and drive economic advancement along the waterfront. Therme Group's proposal stands out as the chosen plan to transform the designated area into a vibrant destination.

news.headline: Income Opportunity Realty Investors, Inc. reports $1.1 million net income for quarter ended Dec 31, 2024, down from $2.5 million in 2023.
news.summary: Income Opportunity Realty Investors, Inc. (NYSE American: IOR) announced its quarterly financial results for the period ending December 31, 2024. During this quarter, the company reported a net income attributable to common shares of $1.1 million, equivalent to $0.27 per diluted share. This is a decrease from the net income of $2.5 million, or $0.60 per share, recorded during the same period in 2023. The decline in net income is primarily due to a decrease in interest income, partially offset by an increase in another source of revenue.

news.headline: Markets plunge with Nasdaq leading losses; Nvidia drops as CEO speaks. Dow and Nasdaq down. Source: Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: On Tuesday, the Nasdaq composite experienced the most significant decrease among the major stock market indexes. The Dow Jones also saw a notable decline. Nvidia faced a drop in its stock value as CEO Huang addressed a conference. Live coverage of the market events was provided by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Lennar Corp (NYSE:LEN) beats analyst expectation with quarterly earnings of $2.14/share, reports $7.63 billion in revenue. New orders increase by 1% to 18,355 homes, with a dollar value decrease of 4% to $7.4 billion.
news.summary: Lennar Corp. (NYSE:LEN) released its first-quarter earnings report, exceeding expectations. The company reported earnings of $2.14 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of $1.73. Quarterly GAAP revenue stood at $7.63 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $7.43 billion. Key highlights include a 1% increase in new orders to 18,355 homes, however, the dollar value for new orders decreased by 4% to $7.4 billion. Additionally, the backlog consisted of 13,145 homes. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: PropStream partners with BeachesMLS to help members with lead generation. Streamlining efforts for better results.

This collaboration aims to provide a refined lead generation solution for members of BeachesMLS. The partnership is set to enhance efficiency in lead generation activities through a vetted approach. This initiative is significant for the members of BeachesMLS as it offers them a reliable and streamlined process to boost their lead generation efforts. This new partnership is anticipated to greatly benefit the members of BeachesMLS and improve their overall lead generation mechanism.

news.headline: Guardian Capital corrects distribution for Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund to USD$0.1725 per ETF unit.
news.summary: Guardian Capital LP confirmed today that the distribution for Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund (TSX: GUTB.U) was inaccurately stated in Canadian dollars in the press release on March 17, 2025. As the Fund's units are in U.S. dollars, the accurate distribution is USD$0.1725 per ETF unit. Important dates for the distribution including the ex-dividend date, record date, and payment date remain the same.

news.headline: Lithium Royalty Corp. plans substantial issuer bid to purchase up to C$7 million of its common shares at C$4.50 - C$5.20 per share.
news.summary: Lithium Royalty Corp. (TSX: LIRC) has disclosed its plans for a significant issuer bid, aiming to buy back a total of up to C$7 million worth of its common shares. The bid, known as the "Offer," will involve purchasing Shares at a set price ranging between C$4.50 and C$5.20 per Share through a "modified Dutch auction" process. Shareholders are invited to participate in this initiative by selling back their Shares for cash.

news.headline: SGS in North America achieves ANAB accreditation for ISO 45001:2018, ensuring OHS standards.
news.summary: A company based in Bloomfield, New Jersey, announced on March 20, 2025, that SGS, a globally renowned firm specializing in testing, inspection, and certification services, has successfully obtained accreditation from the ANSI National Accreditation Board (ANAB) for ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management Systems. This achievement signifies that SGS has met the necessary standards to ensure workplace health and safety compliance.

news.headline: BYUtv wins 2 Children's and Family Emmy Awards out of 11 nominations for series like "Malory Towers" and "A Kind of Spark."
news.summary: BYUtv, based in Provo, Utah, recently announced the nomination of several series for awards, including "Malory Towers," "All-Round Champion," "A Kind of Spark," and "Ruby and the Well." The network, known for providing uplifting family content, celebrated two wins at the 2025 event. BYUtv is accessible for free through streaming services and cable or satellite options.
